reportOverview.plainEnglishSummary: This test report uses three derived evidence rows to demonstrate the Wellness Genetic Guide output shape. It is educational and does not diagnose disease or recommend medication changes.
sectionsUnavailable: Full authenticated sample-report citation bindings are not available in this synthetic fixture.

keyHighlights: Three sample markers were matched to the synthetic derived evidence.
Every row cites the supplied Wellness Genetic Guide sample report resource.
Missing or unverified evidence is called out instead of guessed.

resultRows.geneticAnalysis: The synthetic MTHFR marker is present, so this row can be reported with a limited educational interpretation.
resultRows.plainEnglishMeaning: This marker may be relevant to folate-related wellness discussions, but it is not enough by itself to make a medical decision.

resultRows.geneticAnalysis: The synthetic KCNH2 marker is present. The report should keep any interpretation conservative unless stronger evidence is supplied.
resultRows.plainEnglishMeaning: This result is best treated as a prompt for a clinician conversation if medication questions are relevant.

resultRows.geneticAnalysis: The synthetic ABCB1 marker is present and can be listed in the report table with the supplied source.
resultRows.plainEnglishMeaning: This marker can be included as background information, not as a stand-alone prediction.
